play_screen.story: The Archive is a digital necropolis of human history. Data fragments are drifting, losing their original anchors. Your neural interface is the only tool capable of stabilizing these entities.
play_screen.mission: Identify the historical entity through indirect interaction. You have limited temporal stable windows (attempts). Failure results in data collapse.

about.description: WhoDat is a social deduction experiment designed to test human intuition against reconstructed historical personas. In the age of digital decay, names are the first to vanish. Your objective is to restore the connection.
about.protocol_desc: Each successful identification of a subject grants experience points (XP). Accumulating XP increases your clearance level, unlocking access to deeper archive layers — from Antiquity to the Modern Era.

terms.acceptance: By proceeding, you accept the encryption protocols above.

sections.ai_content: All dialogues are powered by the GPT-4o-mini neural network. The AI may hallucinate, provide historically inaccurate data, or behave provocatively as part of its persona.
sections.ops_content: Users are granted 7 interrogation attempts per session. Inputs are strictly limited to 33 characters to maintain system stability.
sections.data_content: We use Clerk for authentication. Your interrogation history and XP progress are stored in our database in Supabase (PSQL).
sections.warranty_content: This project is provided 'as is'. We do not guarantee 100% uptime. The Archive may go offline for maintenance or due to 'Temporal Distortions'.

chat_field.xp_credited: XP CREDITS TRANSFERRED TO YOUR ACCOUNT

chat_input.disclaimer: AI units may produce anomalous data. Identity verification recommended.
